data.name: ERR_DoesntImplementInterface
data.value: The member '{0}'s return type '{1}' doesn't implement {2}. Either implement '{2}' on {1}, or specify a proxy type.

data.name: ERR_TypeNotPartial
data.value: The type '{0}' has the `[GenerateSerializeAttribute]` applied, but the implementation can't be generated unless the type is marked 'partial'.

data.name: ERR_CantWrapSpecialType
data.value: The type '{0}' can't be automatically wrapped because it is a built-in type.

data.name: ERR_MissingPrimaryCtor
data.value: Type must have either a primary constructor or a parameterless constructor.

data.name: ERR_CantFindNestedWrapper
data.value: Could not find nested type named '{0}' inside type '{1}'. The proxied type '{2}' is generic, so the expected proxy is a parent type with Serialize and Deserialize nested proxies.

data.name: ERR_WrapperDoesntImplementInterface
data.value: The proxy type '{0}' doesn't implement the interface '{2}<{1}>'.

data.name: ERR_CantImplementAbstract
data.value: Can't generate serialize or deserialize implementations for '{0}' because it is abstract but is not a discriminated union. Implementations can only be generated for abstract types if they are discriminated unions. Discriminated unions are: - Abstract - Records - With only private constructors - With one or more nested records that inherit from the parent record

data.name: ERR_CantFindTypeParameter
data.value: Could not find type parameter named '{0}' in the current context.

data.name: ERR_CtorParamMismatch
data.value: Constructor parameter '{0}' on type '{1}' does not match any member by name. All constructor parameters must have a corresponding member with the same name.

data.name: ERR_MissingExplicitConversion
data.value: Proxy type '{0}' must be either empty or define a public explicit conversion operator to '{1}'. Add: public static explicit operator {1}({0} proxy)

data.name: ERR_MissingReverseConversion
data.value: Proxy type '{0}' must define a public explicit conversion operator from '{1}' in order to serialize it. Add: public static explicit operator {0}({1} value)

data.name: ERR_AsTypeNoConversion
data.value: Cannot represent '{0}' as '{1}' because no user-defined conversion from '{0}' to '{1}' was found. Define an explicit or implicit conversion operator between the types.

data.name: ERR_AsTypeNotNamed
data.value: The 'As' option cannot be applied to type '{0}': the 'As' type must be a named type.

data.name: ERR_AsTypeOnEnum
data.value: The 'As' option cannot be applied to enum '{0}'. Use 'AsUnderlying = true' to serialize an enum as its underlying value.

data.name: ERR_AsUnderlyingOnNonEnum
data.value: The 'AsUnderlying' option can only be applied to enums, but '{0}' is not an enum.

data.name: ERR_AsTypeWithOption
data.value: The 'As' option cannot be combined with the '{0}' option.

data.name: ERR_ForTypeUnsupported
data.value: The 'ForType' option cannot be applied to type '{0}': the proxied type must be a named type.

data.name: ERR_WithTypeUnsupported
data.value: The 'With' option cannot be applied to type '{0}': the serde object must be a named type.

data.name: ERR_DuplicateOrdinal
data.value: The explicit 'Ordinal' value '{0}' is assigned to multiple members ('{1}' and '{2}').

data.name: ERR_PartialMemberOrdinal
data.value: Member '{0}' must specify an explicit 'Ordinal' because another member of the type does. When any member sets an explicit ordinal, all members must set one.

data.name: ERR_OrdinalOnSkippedMember
data.value: The 'Ordinal' option on member '{0}' has no effect because the member will not be serialized or deserialized.

data.name: ERR_OrdinalOnEnumMember
data.value: The 'Ordinal' option on enum member '{0}' is not allowed. Enum members are mapped by their declared value, not by ordinal.
